request.request_text: Provide evidence of user access controls, including provisioning, removal of access, and periodic reviews, showing that access is properly approved and revoked when no longer needed.

interpretation.original_request: Provide evidence of user access controls, including provisioning, removal of access, and periodic reviews, showing that access is properly approved and revoked when no longer needed.

interpreted_tasks.task_description: Review access provisioning logs and approval records to confirm users are granted access only after proper authorization and within defined policies, ensuring access is not unduly delayed or denied without justification.
Examine access removal logs to verify that user access is promptly terminated upon role changes, termination, or other reasons for access revocation, per company policy.
Assess results of periodic access reviews to ensure they identify unused or excessive access rights, leading to appropriate corrective actions such as access adjustment or removal.
Verify business process documentation detailing ownership, responsibility, and escalation procedures for user access approvals and revocations, ensuring alignment with organizational governance.

interpretation.interpretation_notes: The primary control assertion being tested is that access to systems and data is appropriately granted and revoked in accordance with organizational policy to mitigate unauthorized access risks.

gap_recommendations: Sufficient: overall sufficiency is 99.8% (>= 85%) based on the strongest 3/7 evidence item(s), including access_control_policy 1.pdf, user_deprovisioning_log.csv, user_provisioning_log.csv. These items collectively provide enough relevance, completeness, and corroboration for the audit objective.
Selected evidence set for sufficiency assessment: 3/7 file(s) -> access_control_policy 1.pdf, user_deprovisioning_log.csv, user_provisioning_log.csv.
Excluded from minimum sufficient set: 4 file(s) -> access_review_report.docx, sod_matrix.csv, approval_email.msg, approval.png.
access_review_report.docx: Not required in the minimum sufficient subset based on comparative contribution.
sod_matrix.csv: Not required in the minimum sufficient subset based on comparative contribution.
approval_email.msg: Not required in the minimum sufficient subset based on comparative contribution.
approval.png: Not required in the minimum sufficient subset based on comparative contribution.
Improvement suggestion: strengthen excluded files by adding explicit control mapping, date coverage, approver/system identifiers, and independent corroborating references.
Verification of timely access revocation thresholds and policy documentation for exception management
Physical evidence of existing documentation (e.g., policy manuals, escalation procedures)
Additional evidence of remediation effectiveness for SoD conflicts beyond reported mitigations
Evidence is sufficient; improve confidence further by adding one corroborating artifact for each major finding.
Map each submitted document to a specific interpreted task/control assertion and document that mapping explicitly.
Add period-spanning evidence (start, middle, end of audit window) to strengthen coverage completeness.
Include independently reviewable artifacts (approvals, logs, exception handling records) for each key control step.
